title: eremalche parryi subsp. parryi
intro: Eremalche parryi subsp. parryi, also known as the Parry's eremalche, is a perennial wildflower native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico, characterized by its delicate pink or white flowers and hairy stems. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it a natural remedy for wound healing and skin irritations. The health benefits of Eremalche parryi subsp. parryi include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory problems. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or salves, and are typically consumed or applied topically to reap its medicinal and health benefits.
